ground_truth.title: What is the most efficient way to learn about real estate? : r/realestateinvesting
ground_truth.author: bizjake
ground_truth.main_content: I'm still in college running on limited funds so I can't learn by purchasing property, but what are some of the resources you used to become proficient in real estate? I've heard many terms such as FHA and DSCR loans, section 8, title companies, escrow, leins, and have a base understanding of them but would like to gain a deeper understanding. My questions is any books you may have read, podcasts, or any other resources that you've used to help you in the business could help me in mine. Thanks again.
with: I'm still in college running on limited funds so I can't learn by purchasing property, but what are some of the resources you used to become proficient in real estate?
I've heard many terms such as FHA and DSCR loans, section 8, title companies, escrow, leins, and have a base understanding of them but would like to gain a deeper understanding.
My questions is any books you may have read, podcasts, or any other resources that you've used to help you in the business could help me in mine.
r/realestateinvesting is focused on sharing thoughts, experiences, advice and encouraging questions regardless of your real estate investing niche!
Interested in Real Estate Investing? You've come to the right place!
